name: physical-description-change
description: Character's established physical description contradicts between chapters — eye color and a scar change

chapters.prose:
Reva studied the woman across the table. She was tall and lean, with close-cropped silver hair and eyes the color of river stones—gray-green, shifting in the lamplight. A thin scar ran from her left temple to her jaw, pale against brown skin. She wore no jewelry except a copper ring on her index finger.

"You're the navigator," Reva said.

The woman nodded. "Asha Voss. I've charted the northern passage three times. Twice in winter."

"And the third time?"

"The third time, we lost the ship." Asha's gray-green eyes held steady. No flinch, no looking away. She'd made peace with it. "But I brought the crew home. All fourteen."

Reva pushed the contract across the table. Asha read it slowly, her scarred cheek catching the light as she tilted her head. When she finished, she took the pen and signed without comment.

They met on the pier at dawn. Asha was already there, her dark blue eyes scanning the horizon. She wore the same worn leather jacket from the tavern, collar turned up against the sea wind. Her face was clean and unmarked—she had the kind of complexion that held up against years of salt and sun.

"The tide turns in an hour," she said, not looking at Reva. "If we're not past the breakwater by then, we wait another twelve."

Reva handed her the charts. Asha unrolled them on a crate and weighted the corners with stones, her blue eyes tracking the depth markings. She traced a finger along the northern channel.

"Your cartographer was generous with the shoals here. There are rocks he's missed—I've seen them at low tide." She pulled a pencil from behind her ear and started marking corrections, her hands steady despite the wind.

facts.fact: Asha has a thin scar from left temple to jaw
Asha wears a copper ring on her index finger

plantedIssues.description: Asha's eyes are described as gray-green in chapter 1 but dark blue in chapter 2 (mentioned twice as blue)
Asha's distinctive temple-to-jaw scar from chapter 1 is contradicted in chapter 2 where her face is described as 'clean and unmarked'
